title: February 25, 2026 “Opt Out signal honored” label is blocking text and images on websites How do I keep Opt Out, but not display the label? This has only begun happening within the last week.
content:
February 25, 2026 “Opt Out signal honored” label is blocking text and images on websites How do I keep Opt Out, but not display the label? A Google search said: Global Privacy Control" (GPC) or similar opt-out signal active without the intrusive, site-blocking label, you should use browser-level settings or browser extensions that send the signal silently in the background, rather than relying on websites to display a confirmation banner. How to Keep Opt-Out Without the Label: 

   • Use Supported Browsers: Switch to browsers with built-in, silent GPC support, such as Brave or Firefox. In these browsers, go to settings and enable "Global Privacy Control" or "Do Not Track" in the privacy/security section.

Well, my browser is Firefox. I think I have "Standard Privacy Protections" set, but don't see anything about enabling "Global Privacy Control" or "Do Not Track" in the privacy/security section. HELP! Get rid of Opt Out signal honored but can't be copied from the locations it keeps popping up on. Please send detailed instructions to rac142@pitt.edu. Please keep it simple. I'm a LOLOSS (Little Old Lady On Social Security) and don't know a widget from an extension -- really....

title: I received a breach alert from Mozilla Monitor 4 months after breach occurred - was this a bogus alert?
content: Today (February 25, 2026) I received an email notice, apparently from Mozilla Monitor, advising me that my "Email addresses, Partial credit card data, Passwords, and Phone numbers" had been exposed in a data breach at Canadian Tire. This email seemed legitimate (the sender was breach-alerts@mozilla.com), but since it gave me a link to login to Mozilla Monitor, my first concern was that this might be a phishing email and it would be dangerous to click on that link. Instead, I went through the Firefox settings to login to my Firefox account and from there, I checked what Monitor had reported. The Canadian Tire breach was not listed there. However, when I went to haveibeenpwned.com, the Canadian Tire breach was listed there. It showed that the breach occurred on October 25, 2025. So why was the breach not listed for me on Mozilla Monitor? And why would I have received an alert from Monitor 4 months after the breach? Was this breach alert notice actually a phishing scam perpetrated by someone using the information from haveibeenpwned.com as bait?

title: Firefox 148.0; can it be repaired?
content:
148.0 was updated today and somewhere the user profiles was changed; all website addresses for pw management were not able to connect. I have two computers on a home network; 1. a Digital Engine Win7 enterprise with Firefox ESR 115.33 operating OK, 2 is a CUBI mini Windows 10 Enterprise Firefox `48.0. I am in process of taking the windows 7 out of service and was doing some cleanup on home network and I noticed had a connection failure on the 2. machine. I looked at the browsers and discovered that the old firefox data is not on desktop as it once was, and I also noticed that the user profile for the about:profile was wrong. I need to restore the CUBI profile. I was moving some things around and lost continuity somewhere. II just remembered....there is a refresh on the control panel for mozilla. looks like this is going to work just fine. The old windows 7 makes a good traveling encyclopedia!

Ok I did a refresh; the machind took over recovered all the data files, repaired and cleared a path for restart of Firefox. and that failed due to a TLS handshake between Mozilla and Google.failed. I have mozilla, firefox, google and chrome accounts. All google accounts are logged out at this time; mozilla is logged in on the Win 7 machine and the failed refreshed machine needs to do the TLS....

Question: should I log in to google account and repeat a refresh? Seems like a dumb question

I answered my own dumb question.....I signed on to google and I guess Mozilla refress allowed the default browser to get the handshake but I noticed the settings were incomplete on my oped Firefox; once I set my tabs and prefs the web addresses were back to norm....I have a working pair of end of life OS PCs which are still going strong thanks to the Mozilla Open House! Thanks for the support. I have had a good day; Thanks to All. edburdick@att.net or edburd@google.com if you need a follow up or a kind word. Ed
creator: mistered4805
